userSaved.title: Child’s Play, by Sam Kriss
userSaved.snippet: Tech’s new generation and the end of thinking

highlights.highlight: As far as he was concerned, the fact that tech people were tripping over themselves to take part in his stunt just confirmed his generally low impression of them. “They have too much money and nothing going on. They have no swag, no smoke, no motion, no hoes. That’s all you need to know.”
All he wanted, he’d said, was to hang out with his friends.

All he wanted, he’d said, was to hang out with his friends. I believed him. He wanted not to be alone, the way he’d been alone for a year after having his offer of admission rescinded by Harvard. For people to pay attention to him. To exist for other people. But instead of making friends the normal way, he’d walked up to strangers and asked whether they wanted to start a company with him, and then he built the most despised startup in San Francisco.

userSaved.title: The Idealists Collective
userSaved.snippet: we are the SF counterculture. we are embracing the new world of infinite software. we will not be satisfied with any direction other than towards utopia. we are, first and foremost, idealists. ✺✺✺✺✺ we are not afraid to be ambitious in our visions of the future. and we are not afraid to try to make it a reality. you control your tools, your data, your attention. decisions happen at the level where they matter. we create with whimsy and joy. what is life without a little fun? dynamic systems over static entities. things that grow, adapt, merge, and die when they need to. we cannot succeed alone. nor would we want to. don't compromise on your integrity. don't sell your soul. do everything with love.

userSaved.title: Socratica Symposium
userSaved.snippet: You are invited to Socratica Symposium - the world’s greatest celebration of making. Join us on March 21-22 in the Region of Waterloo. Socratica is an interdisciplinary maker collective formed at the University of Waterloo, where creatives of all kinds meet weekly to work on passion projects. Symposium is an annual exhibition of their work, spanning art, science, technology, and everything in between.

userSaved.title: how to avoid half-heartedness - by Ava - bookbear express
userSaved.snippet: A few nights ago a friend and I were talking about the moment when someone shows you their texts with their crush and asks you to analyze it with them, i.e.

highlights.highlight: If you want to be loved, find something you love. People can sense it when you have something you’re dedicated to. No one wants the burden of being the answer to your dissatisfication. When you’re unsure of yourself, it’s easy to be obsessed with the idea of love—the idea that happiness will arrive when someone else loves you. This can lead to you ignoring your own life.

userSaved.title: I no longer believe that it’s possible to achieve extremely high productivity sustained over long periods of time working on difficult projects alone, so now I spend the majority of my working time co-working with my friends over video in my virtual gather.town office - Alexey Guzey
userSaved.snippet:
Starting March 2021, I’ve been spending the majority of my working time co-working with my friends over video in my virtual gather.town office.
The sessions are usually pre-scheduled, last 2-16 hours, and we do brief check-ins every 30 minutes.
Most people I co-worked with told me that they really enjoyed the experience and that they were a lot more productive than usual. Several people I did a 10 or 12 hour session with told me afterwards that they didn’t realize it’s possible …
